tasks.title: Fix @unchecked Sendable data race risks in core classes
tasks.description: Workspace, RalphCLIRun, and QueueFileWatcher use @unchecked Sendable to suppress Swift concurrency warnings. This is unsafe as these classes have mutable shared state that could cause data races. Proper concurrency isolation with @MainActor or actors should be implemented.
evidence: Workspace.swift line 24: 'public final class Workspace: @unchecked Sendable'
RalphCLIClient.swift line 67: 'public final class RalphCLIRun: @unchecked Sendable'
QueueFileWatcher.swift line 23: 'public final class QueueFileWatcher: @unchecked Sendable'
These suppress concurrency safety without proper isolation
plan: Enable strict concurrency checking in Xcode build settings
Refactor Workspace to use @MainActor for all @Published properties
Convert RalphCLIRun to use actor isolation for mutable state
Apply @MainActor to QueueFileWatcher callback handlers
Replace NSLock usage with actor or Sendable constructs where appropriate
Run Thread Sanitizer to verify no data races remain
Fix all compiler warnings for concurrency
Verify by: build with SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY=complete, no warnings

tasks.title: Add CLI version compatibility check on app launch
tasks.description: The macOS app does not verify the bundled ralph CLI version on launch. If the CLI is incompatible (wrong version, corrupted, or missing), the app will fail unpredictably during operations instead of showing a clear error at startup. Version checking should happen early with actionable guidance.
evidence: RalphMacApp.swift lines 215-221: Creates workspace without CLI validation
No version parsing or compatibility matrix implemented
E2E test only checks version exists, not compatibility
plan: Define supported CLI version range (e.g., 0.1.0 - 0.2.0)
Add version check in WorkspaceManager initialization
Parse 'ralph --version' output and compare against supported range
Show blocking alert if CLI is incompatible with download/reinstall options
Add 'Check for Updates' menu item for manual version check
Cache version check result to avoid repeated calls
Write tests for version parsing and compatibility logic
Verify by: launch app with incompatible CLI version, verify warning appears

tasks.title: Implement ANSI escape code parsing for colored console output
tasks.description: Workspace.parseANSICodes() is completely stubbed - it just returns the raw text without parsing any ANSI escape sequences. The console shows raw escape codes (e.g., '[32m') instead of colored text, making output hard to read. A proper ANSI parser needs to be implemented.
evidence: Workspace.swift lines 1000-1029: parseANSICodes() creates single unstyled segment
Line 1005-1012: Comment says 'For now, create a single segment with default styling'
Console output shows raw ANSI codes like '[32m' instead of green text
plan: Implement ANSI escape sequence parser supporting SGR codes (colors, bold, italic)
Support 8 standard colors, bright variants, and 256-color codes
Handle reset codes (0m) properly
Support cursor movement codes for progress bars (or strip them)
Update ANSISegment struct to support all parsed attributes
Write unit tests for various ANSI sequences
Test with actual ralph CLI output containing colors
Verify by: run 'ralph run one', verify console shows colored output not raw codes

tasks.title: Fix O(N²) task comparison performance issue in change detection
tasks.description: Workspace.detectTaskChanges() uses nested loops with first(where:) lookups resulting in O(N²) time complexity. For large task lists (500+ tasks), this causes UI stutter when file watching detects changes. The method should use Dictionary-based lookups for O(N) performance.
evidence: Workspace.swift lines 464-485: detectTaskChanges uses nested loops with first(where:)
Line 474: previous.first(where:) inside loop over current = O(N²)
File watcher triggers this on every queue.json change - performance degrades with task count
plan: Refactor detectTaskChanges to use Dictionary for O(1) lookups
Create [String: RalphTask] maps for both previous and current tasks
Compare using dictionary lookups instead of array scanning
Add performance test with 1000+ tasks to verify O(N) behavior
Consider adding @MainActor isolation to prevent concurrent modifications
Profile with Instruments to verify performance improvement
Verify by: create 1000 tasks, modify externally, verify UI updates without stutter

tasks.title: Add console output size limit to prevent memory exhaustion
tasks.description: RunControlConsoleView accumulates all CLI output in memory without any size limit. Long-running tasks or tasks with verbose output can consume excessive memory, potentially causing the app to crash or be killed by the system. The console should implement a rolling buffer with configurable limits.
evidence: Workspace.swift line 30: @Published public var output: String - grows unbounded
RunControlConsoleView.swift lines 67-80: All output accumulated in workspace.output
No truncation or rolling buffer implemented for long-running processes
plan: Implement CircularOutputBuffer with configurable max lines (default 10,000)
Add max total characters limit (default 10MB) with overflow indicator
Add 'Clear Output' button to manually free memory
Optionally save overflow to temp file for full log access
Add visual indicator when output is truncated
Write tests for buffer overflow behavior
Verify by: run task with massive output, verify memory stays bounded

tasks.title: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for transient CLI failures
tasks.description: CLI operations that fail due to transient issues (file locking, temporary IO errors) are not retried, causing operations to fail unnecessarily. The app should implement automatic retry with exponential backoff for recoverable errors, improving reliability without user intervention.
evidence: RalphCLIClient.swift lines 340-364: runAndCollect fails immediately on any error
Workspace.swift updateTaskStatus (lines 622-653): No retry for transient failures
Queue file locking conflicts cause immediate failures without retry
plan: Define retryable error types (file locked, IO timeout, temporary unavailable)
Implement retry helper with exponential backoff (100ms, 200ms, 400ms, 800ms, 1600ms)
Add max retry count (default 3) with user-visible progress
Apply retry to critical operations: loadTasks, updateTaskStatus, createTask
Add jitter to prevent thundering herd in multi-instance scenarios
Write tests simulating transient failures
Verify by: simulate file lock during operation, verify automatic retry succeeds

tasks.title: Add XCUITest coverage for critical user flows
tasks.description: The macOS app has only 3 unit tests and 0 XCUITests. Critical user flows (task creation, editing, status changes, execution control) have no automated UI testing. This allows regressions to go undetected and requires manual testing for every release.
evidence: Only 3 test files exist: RalphCLIClientTests, RalphModelsTests, RalphE2ESmokeTests
0 XCUITest files - no UI automation coverage
Critical flows (create task, edit task, run task, kanban drag-drop) untested
plan: Create RalphMacUITests target with XCUITest bundle
Add test: Create new task via UI and verify it appears in list
Add test: Edit task title and verify change persists
Add test: Change task status via Kanban drag-drop
Add test: Run next task and verify console output appears
Add test: Switch between view modes (list, kanban, graph)
Add test: Create and switch between multiple workspaces
Integrate UI tests into make macos-ci target
Verify by: run make macos-ci, all UI tests pass

tasks.title: Replace print statements with structured logging and add crash reporting
tasks.description: The macOS app uses print() for debug output which is lost in production and not visible to users. There is no structured logging system or crash reporting mechanism, making it impossible to diagnose issues in the field. This should be replaced with os.log and a crash reporter.
evidence: QueueFileWatcher.swift lines 103, 111, 118: Uses print() for logging
No os.log or Logger usage found in the codebase
No crash reporting framework or custom crash handler implemented
plan: Replace all print() statements with os.Logger appropriate categories
Create RalphLogger helper with subsystem 'com.mitchfultz.ralph'
Implement log levels: debug, info, error, fault
Add crash reporter (PLCrashReporter or Sentry) for release builds
Add 'Export Logs' feature in Help menu for user support
Ensure logs don't contain sensitive data (redact paths if needed)
Write tests verifying logs are created at appropriate levels
Verify by: run app, check Console.app for structured Ralph logs

tasks.title: Implement workspace directory passing from CLI to macOS app
tasks.description: When running 'ralph app open', the CLI should pass the current working directory to the macOS app, and the app should set its workspace to that directory. This requires implementing a custom URL scheme for IPC between the CLI and the SwiftUI app.
evidence: User request: 'If the user runs 'ralph app open' the macos app should open and set the workspace to the directory the command was run in'
Current 'ralph app open' only launches the app without passing workspace context
WorkspaceManager.createWorkspace(workingDirectory:) already supports custom directories
No URL scheme handling exists in the macOS app yet
plan: Register custom URL scheme 'ralph://' in macOS app Info.plist
Add onOpenURL handler in RalphMacApp.swift to handle incoming URL events
Implement URL parsing in macOS app to extract workspace path from 'ralph://open?workspace=/path'
Modify workspace creation logic to use the passed directory when opening via URL
Update CLI AppOpenArgs to capture current working directory
Modify CLI app open command to launch app then send URL with workspace path
Handle case where app is already running - URL should be delivered to existing instance
Add error handling for invalid/unreadable workspace paths
Write tests for CLI URL generation logic
Verify by running 'ralph app open' from different directories and confirming workspace is set correctly
tasks.request: If the user runs 'ralph app open' the macos app should open and set the workspace to the directory the command was run in.

tasks.title: Fix window state not restored on app relaunch
tasks.description: The macOS app does not restore window state (workspace directories, selected tabs, navigation state) when the app is quit and relaunched. WindowState persistence exists but is not being applied on app startup. This degrades user experience as users must manually reopen their workspaces each time.
evidence: WindowState.swift exists for persistence but WindowViewContainer always creates fresh state
RalphMacApp.swift lines 199-223: WindowViewContainer creates new workspace on every launch
No NSWindowRestoration or SwiftUI window restoration implemented
plan: Implement window restoration using NSWindowRestoration protocol
Modify WindowViewContainer to load saved WindowState from UserDefaults on launch
Save window state (size, position, workspace directories) on app termination
Add restoration for NavigationViewModel state (selected section, view mode)
Ensure state is saved when workspaces are added/removed/renamed
Write unit tests for state save/load round-trip
Verify by: open app, open multiple workspaces, quit, relaunch - state should restore

tasks.title: Add keyboard navigation support in task lists and Kanban board
tasks.description: The task list and Kanban board views lack full keyboard navigation support. Users cannot navigate between tasks using arrow keys, select tasks with Enter/Space, or perform actions via keyboard shortcuts. This makes the app inaccessible to keyboard-only users and reduces productivity for power users.
evidence: TaskListView.swift (507 lines): No keyboard event handling for arrow keys
KanbanBoardView.swift (143 lines): No keyboard navigation between columns or cards
No focus management or onKeyPress handlers in list/card views
plan: Add FocusState management to track currently focused task
Implement arrow key navigation (↑↓) in TaskListView
Implement arrow key navigation (←→↑↓) between Kanban columns and cards
Add Enter key to open selected task detail
Add Space key for quick preview (if applicable)
Add Cmd+Enter to start work on selected task
Ensure focus ring is visible for all interactive elements
Write XCUITest to verify keyboard navigation works
Verify by: use Tab to enter list, arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select

tasks.title: Add dependency cycle detection warning in graph view
tasks.description: The DependencyGraphView does not detect or warn users about circular dependencies (e.g., Task A depends on B, B depends on C, C depends on A). Cycles can cause infinite loops in task processing and make the queue state invalid. The graph should visually highlight cycles and show a warning.
evidence: DependencyGraphView.swift lines 133-150: Creates edges without cycle detection
No cycle detection algorithm implemented in graph handling
Circular dependencies would cause undefined behavior in task execution order
plan: Implement cycle detection using DFS (Tarjan's algorithm or simple visited set)
Add cycle visualization - highlight edges involved in cycle in red
Add warning banner when cycles are detected
Add cycle details showing the chain of dependencies forming the cycle
Consider blocking status changes that would create cycles
Write unit tests for cycle detection with various graph shapes
Verify by: create tasks with circular dependencies, open graph view, verify warning shows

tasks.title: Add conflict detection for external task changes during editing
tasks.description: TaskDetailView does not detect when a task has been modified externally (via CLI or another app instance) while the user is editing it. This can lead to data loss when the user saves, overwriting the external changes. The app should warn users about conflicts and offer merge options.
evidence: TaskDetailView.swift lines 31-42: Draft task created from initial task, no update tracking
File watching triggers reload but doesn't check if user is currently editing
No mechanism to compare task version at save time vs. edit start time
plan: Store original task checksum/version when editing begins
On file change notification, compare current task with draft
Show conflict alert if task changed externally with options: overwrite, discard changes, or merge
Implement merge view showing diff between local and external changes
Add 'modified externally' indicator in UI when conflict detected
Write tests for conflict detection scenarios
Verify by: open task in app, modify via CLI, attempt save in app - conflict should appear

tasks.title: Add error recovery UI for failed CLI operations
tasks.description: When CLI operations fail (network issues, corrupted queue, permission errors), the app shows a simple error message but offers no recovery options. Users cannot retry, view detailed error logs, or get guidance on fixing the issue. This leaves users stuck when errors occur.
evidence: WorkspaceView.swift lines 284-290: Simple error display with no recovery actions
TaskListView.swift lines 225-232: Error shown but no retry mechanism
All error handling shows static text without actionable recovery steps
plan: Create reusable ErrorRecoveryView with retry, diagnose, and dismiss actions
Add detailed error logging with 'Copy Error Details' button
Implement retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
Add 'Diagnose' button that runs ralph queue validate and shows results
Show contextual help based on error type (permission, parse, network)
Add 'Open in Terminal' button to debug CLI issues manually
Write tests for error recovery flows
Verify by: induce CLI error (bad permissions), verify recovery UI appears with retry option

tasks.title: Add offline mode detection and graceful degradation
tasks.description: The macOS app does not detect when the bundled ralph CLI is unavailable or when the workspace directory is inaccessible. The app shows loading spinners indefinitely or crashes without clear feedback. The app should detect offline/unavailable state and show appropriate UI.
evidence: RalphCLIClient.swift lines 265-278: Validates executable but no ongoing availability checks
Workspace.swift loadTasks() hangs indefinitely if CLI fails to respond
No timeout handling for CLI operations in the UI layer
plan: Implement CLI health check on app launch and workspace switch
Add timeout (30s) for all CLI operations with user-cancelable progress
Create OfflineBannerView to show when CLI is unavailable
Add 'Retry Connection' button with visual feedback
Cache last known task list for offline viewing (read-only mode)
Show appropriate messages for different failure modes (no CLI, no workspace, permission denied)
Write tests for offline behavior simulation
Verify by: remove/move ralph binary, launch app - should show offline state with retry

tasks.title: Add menu bar extra for quick task access and status
tasks.description: Implement a menu bar extra (status bar item) that provides quick access to Ralph without opening the full app. Show current task status, next task title, and quick actions (run next, add quick task, open full app). This enables lightweight interaction for users who want to monitor and control Ralph from anywhere.
evidence: No menu bar integration exists - app must be open to see status
Menu bar extras are standard for background/utility apps on macOS
Users may want quick status without full app window
Menu bar provides always-available access
plan: Create MenuBarExtra using App protocol or NSStatusBar
Add Ralph icon to menu bar with status indicator
Show current/next task title in menu
Add 'Run Next Task' menu item with keyboard shortcut
Add 'Quick Add Task' menu item that opens input dialog
Show task counts by status (Todo: 5, Doing: 1, Done: 12)
Add 'Open Full App' menu item
Implement preferences toggle to enable/disable menu bar extra
Show notifications for task completion via menu bar
Add recent tasks submenu for quick access
Verify by clicking menu bar icon and testing all menu items

tasks.title: Implement global keyboard shortcuts and command palette
tasks.description: Add comprehensive keyboard shortcuts for power users including Cmd+N (new task), Cmd+R (run next), Cmd+K (command palette), Cmd+1/2/3 (switch views). Implement a command palette (Cmd+Shift+P or Cmd+K) that provides quick access to all app actions with fuzzy search. This enables efficient keyboard-driven workflows.
evidence: No keyboard shortcuts exist beyond system defaults
Power users expect keyboard-driven workflows
Command palettes are standard in modern dev tools (VS Code, Raycast)
Shortcuts significantly improve productivity for frequent actions
plan: Define keyboard shortcut scheme (Cmd+N new, Cmd+R run, Cmd+F find, Cmd+K palette)
Implement keyboard shortcut handlers in window
Create CommandPaletteView with fuzzy search
Register all app actions in palette with descriptions
Add shortcut indicators in menu items and tooltips
Implement view switching shortcuts (Cmd+1 Queue, Cmd+2 Run, Cmd+3 Analytics)
Add escape key handling to dismiss modals/palette
Implement space key to preview selected task
Add shortcuts for status changes (Cmd+Shift+S for status menu)
Document all shortcuts in Help menu
Verify by testing each shortcut and confirming action fires
